opp.next_step: 5/4/26 RH F/up Ben on order management. Invitation to AI Virtual Global Summit to BEn Zhao and Miranda Carrington. 4/23/26 RH discussed accuracy of previous pricing from last year. Clarifying question on Ben's definition of Order Management.- Pending response.
opp.ais_why:
• World Bank's RFI lists AI-driven demand forecasting and scenario analysis under Demand and Procurement Planning, plus AI/ML-driven classification under Spend Analytics — explicit AI requirements.
• The opportunity itself is named around iSourcing, iSupplier, and Merlin Intake, with rep extending an AI Virtual Global Summit invite to Ben Zhao and Miranda Carrington — Merlin Intake is the anchor.
• Last activity May 3, 2026 with a consistent AI cadence from Richard Hunsinger — aligns cleanly with Hungry.

items.area: RFP timeline promised for calendar 2025 never confirmed as launched

items.area: No direct engagement with the economic buyer (Kenny K. Cheung) or executive sponsor (Gallina Vincelette)

metrics.narrative: The buyer articulated a clear transformation problem but never quantified it. Benjamin Zhu framed it on 03 Feb 2025 as 'a transformational solution... a platform that is going to manage large volume of procurement for the World Bank Group' spanning all entities (IBRD, IFC, MIGA, ICSID), with ~120 core sourcing/category managers and ~6,000 TTL project managers who self-service requests through intake (01 Jul 2025). The operational pain is fragmented tooling across six functionalities and a manual intake-review-resubmit-approve cycle he described on 01 Jul 2025. However, no savings, cycle-time, headcount, or error-rate number and no ROI target was stated on any call, so the value case is qualitative only. Quantifying it into a CFO-grade business case tied to the 120 users and 6,000 TTLs is the outstanding move.

champion.narrative: There is no confirmed internal champion. Benjamin Zhu is the engaged buyer project lead who orchestrated the RFI, coordinated both demos and gave positive AI/Teams feedback, but he operates as a neutral, process-bound evaluator ('the RFI is not a selection process', 'we debrief after the whole process completes', 01 Jul 2025) with no evidence of internal advocacy, budget access, or blocking rivals for Zycus. He has now gone quiet since his 23 Apr 2026 order-management email, with rep follow-ups on 24 Apr and 03 May 2026 unanswered. Miranda Carington (Project Manager) and Timothy Gross (Global Category Team Head) are named in contact roles but show no advocacy. Converting Benjamin Zhu's positive sentiment into an active champion, or building one nearer the economic buyer, is the top relationship move.

competition.narrative: Zycus is competing inside a deliberately multi-vendor RFI, but the competitive set has never been disclosed. Benjamin Zhu was explicit on 03 Feb 2025 that this is 'a market exploration', 'a blue ocean who offers what', that 'we cannot afford inviting everyone to the demo', and that the RFI is not a prequalification. Zycus earned two detailed demos and positive AI/Teams feedback, which signals it cleared the shortlist, but no rival was ever named in any call, field, next step or task, so no competitor can be listed without fabricating one. Practically Zycus should assume live suite competition for a five-entity international institution. Surfacing who else advanced (by asking Benjamin Zhu directly) is a prerequisite to a real win strategy.

sources: OpportunityContactRole: Kenny K. Cheung, Timothy Gross
identify_pain.narrative: The pain is a fragmented procurement operation across all World Bank Group entities that the bank wants to consolidate onto one transformational platform (03 Feb 2025). Specific confirmed pain: no demand-planning capability today, a manual intake-review-resubmit-approve loop for ~6,000 TTL project managers, and a need for guided, self-service intake so business users can run procurement tasks themselves (01 Jul 2025, Benjamin Zhu). The pain owner is the corporate procurement function led by Kenny K. Cheung, with category managers (Timothy Gross's team) as day-to-day users. Inferred pain (from stack and RFI breadth): integration complexity and duplication in supplier onboarding. Pain is well established qualitatively; it has simply never been costed.

paper_process.narrative: Contracting mechanics are partly visible from the RFI stage. A mutual NDA (RFI 25-0304) was in play on 03 Feb 2025, with Benjamin Zhu flagging World Bank-specific legal terms: privileges and immunities as an international organization and dispute resolution by arbitration rather than U.S. federal court, with 'very limited flexibility to change this document'. This signals a rigid, standardized paper process that Zycus legal must be primed for. Beyond the NDA, the MSA/SOW, InfoSec review and procurement governance sit inside the not-yet-launched RFP and are unknown. SI/implementation-partner strategy is undecided. What would create visibility is confirmation of the RFP timeline and the internal governance/approval chain from Benjamin Zhu or the procurement office.

sources: OpportunityContactRole: Kenny K. Cheung, Gallina Vincelette
economic_buyer.narrative: Budget authority is mapped by role but not yet worked directly. Kenny K. Cheung is the Chief of Procurement and Director - Budget, Performance Review and Strategic Planning (OpportunityContactRole), the natural economic buyer for a procurement platform purchase, and Gallina Vincelette (Vice President) is the executive sponsor tier. Benjamin Zhu confirmed the commercial gate on 01 Jul 2025 and 03 Feb 2025: any award depends on internal solutioning, deployment/IT strategy and that 'budget needs to be consolidated', and pricing detail would only come in the RFP. No direct EB-level conversation on budget, ROI or commercial terms has been recorded; the deal is single-threaded through Benjamin Zhu. Securing a direct line to Cheung and Vincelette before the RFP is the key power move.

decision_process.narrative: The buyer laid out an explicit multi-stage process: RFI responses (due 20 Feb 2025) then internal review, selective vendor demos, then a formal RFP that 'may be launched in 2025 calendar year', followed by detailed requirements, security review, internal governance and contract award (03 Feb 2025, 01 Jul 2025). Benjamin Zhu was clear the RFI 'is not a selection process' and that debriefs come only 'after the whole process completes'. World Bank's June fiscal year-end shapes timing, and SI/system-integrator involvement (Deloitte/KPMG/Accenture-type) is open but undecided (03 Feb 2025). The risk is process drift: the promised 2025 RFP has not appeared and the current process stage is unconfirmed after 12+ months of no calls, so re-confirming where the RFP stands is essential.

decision_criteria.narrative: The evaluation criteria are well documented from the RFI. Benjamin Zhu named six core functionality asks the tool must satisfy: demand and procurement planning (with what-if/seasonality analysis), spend analytics, supplier onboarding including duplication check, supplier risk and performance evaluation, category management, and self-service guided intake for ~6,000 TTLs (01 Jul 2025). He explicitly weighted AI capability and Microsoft Teams-native intake ('we use Teams very much', 'the AI component was something interesting', 01 Jul 2025) and stressed persona resonance for the procurement officer/specialist. Security and IT compatibility with World Bank requirements are formal criteria raised on 03 Feb 2025. Criteria are confirmed; how Zycus scores against rivals on them is not, because the competitive set is undisclosed.

cro_panel.intro: One read per score, in language a CRO/CO can act on. We're behind on this one — the reads below say why and what would change it.

blocks.read: We're well behind — this one is cold.
blocks.title: Zycus win position

bullets.full: Losing on capability fit — AIS 7.0 / Moderate. The calls corroborate genuine AI appetite (buyer called out the AI component and Teams intake as interesting on 01 Jul 2025) and the RFI names explicit AI requirements, so the field looks fairly rated to slightly under-rated versus the buyer's stated interest.
bullets.text: Losing on capability fit — AIS 7.0 / Moderate.

bullets.full: Buyer is leaning our way — Positive but non-decisive: 'we use Teams very much, so this is definitely an interesting solution' and 'the AI component was something interesting' (01 Jul 2025), stated as an off-the-record initial impression during a non-selection RFI; no recent (2026) re-confirmation, so this signal is decaying.

Benjamin Zhu is the engaged buyer project lead who ran the RFI, coordinated both demos and gave positive AI/Teams feedback, but he behaves as a neutral, process-bound evaluator with no evidence of internal advocacy or budget access, and he has been silent since 23 Apr 2026 with two rep follow-ups unanswered. What has developed: consistent access and positive product sentiment; what is missing: proof of internal advocacy, EB access, and any push on Zycus's behalf. The move is to give him a reason to champion (peer reference, quantified value model, a fresh AI capability) and secure a path to Kenny K. Cheung.
bullets.text: No real champion yet — No confirmed champion.

bullets.full: A competitor is pushing back — No competitor is named in any source: Competitors__c is blank and no rival appears in the two transcripts, next steps, tasks or field history. This is NOT a sole-source deal, though: Benjamin Zhu ran a deliberate multi-vendor RFI ('a market exploration', 'who offers what', 'we cannot afford inviting everyone to the demo', 03 Feb 2025), so Zycus is competing against an undisclosed set. Zycus cleared into two detailed demos and drew positive feedback on Merlin AI and Microsoft Teams-native intake ('we use Teams very much... the AI component was something interesting', 01 Jul 2025), indicating strong RFI positioning. The single most important competitive action is to surface the shortlist by asking Benjamin Zhu who else advanced; until then the current threat is UNKNOWN and no rival should be asserted. Zycus wedge: Merlin Intake for Teams, the autonomous-negotiation agent, and full-suite breadth covering all six RFI functionalities versus likely point-solution rivals.

blocks.title: What moves it forward

cro_panel.header: World Bank Group — Qualified · $550K · closes 4 Nov

commentary.deal_risk: Risk 14 is driven by Verdict 'Slowing' — momentum stalling. Only observed negatives count, so these are real warning signs, not gaps.
commentary.win_position: Win 17 is carried by preference strength +0.40 (weight 20); exec access strength +0.30 (weight 15). Offsetting it: differentiation strength -0.40 (weight 20).
commentary.deal_momentum: Momentum 52 (roughly flat): Next Step carries 2 dated milestone(s) — actively worked. Silence alone was not treated as backward motion.
commentary.evidence_coverage: Partial Read: 4 of 7 evidence dimensions seen (engagement, stage integrity, exec and champion, forward motion). It labels how much of the picture we have, never the primary scores.
commentary.customer_commitment: Commitment sits near the earned-from-zero floor: little observable customer investment surfaced. Commitment is earned, so a low value is honest, not a penalty.
commentary.forecast_confidence: Forecast confidence 2 is anchored on the stage win (17), nudged by commitment 8 and momentum 52, and dragged by risk 14. Read is Partial Read — a confidence flag (we know less), not a haircut on the score.

contributions.evidence: Next Step carries 2 dated milestone(s) — actively worked.

The calls corroborate genuine AI appetite (buyer called out the AI component and Teams intake as interesting on 01 Jul 2025) and the RFI names explicit AI requirements, so the field looks fairly rated to slightly under-rated versus the buyer's stated interest.

business_case.evidence: Qualitative transformation pain across all World Bank Group entities with ~120 core users and ~6,000 TTLs, but no ROI, savings, cycle-time or headcount number stated on any call.

Close date pushed 21 Aug 2026 to 04 Nov 2026 by Richard Hunsinger

deal_movement.summary: Momentum built through the 2025 RFI/demo cycle then flattened. After two demos and positive AI/Teams feedback in mid-2025, engagement narrowed to email: the owner raised an implementation estimate and pricing accuracy, Benjamin Zhu replied on 23 Apr 2026 asking whether Order Management is in scope, and rep follow-ups on 24 Apr and 03 May 2026 (including an AI Virtual Global Summit invite) drew no response. Amount was raised 400k to 550k on 30 Mar 2026 and close date pushed from 21 Aug to 04 Nov 2026 on 18 Feb 2026, both rep-side moves, not buyer signals.

The budget owner, Chief of Procurement Kenny K. Cheung, and VP sponsor Gallina Vincelette are named but have never been engaged, leaving the deal single-threaded through one evaluator.
This is a deliberate multi-vendor RFI yet no rival has ever been named, so the field is unknown and must be surfaced before any win strategy is real.
A full-suite $550k scope is on the table but budget still needs consolidation and no value case has been quantified, so the price is not yet justified to power.

confidence_signals.summary: Engagement has thinned to a single evaluator who has gone quiet for ~70 days; positive 2025 sentiment has not been re-confirmed and the promised RFP has not materialized.

north_star_verdict.math: Created 29 Jan 2025, qualified 20 Feb 2025; ~499 days in Qualified as of 03 Jul 2026 (very extended, though a 12-15 month RFI-to-RFP cycle for a five-entity international institution is not abnormal). Last buyer reply 23 Apr 2026 (Benjamin Zhu email on Order Management), ~71 days ago; rep follow-ups 24 Apr and 03 May 2026 unanswered. Last recorded buyer call 01 Jul 2025. Close date 04 Nov 2026 is 124 days out. Buyer targeted an end-of-summer-2025 RFI conclusion and a possible calendar-2025 RFP (01 Jul 2025 call); ~10 months past that milestone with no RFP and no confirmed re-engagement. Pipeline forecast is defensible; close date holds only if the buyer re-engages and confirms RFP timing within ~14-21 days.
evidence: Two deep-read transcripts show strong RFI-stage engagement: 03 Feb 2025 NDA/RFI 25-0304 kickoff and 01 Jul 2025 demo-planning call, both led by buyer project lead Benjamin Zhu, covering the six core RFI functionalities (demand and procurement planning, spend analytics, supplier onboarding with duplication check, supplier risk and performance, category management, self-service intake).

Positive product reception on 01 Jul 2025: Benjamin Zhu said 'we use Teams very much, so this is definitely an interesting solution' and recalled 'the AI component' as 'something interesting' (off-the-record initial impression), validating the Merlin Intake for Teams and autonomous-negotiation wedge.

Buyer decision process (03 Feb 2025 and 01 Jul 2025): RFI responses due 20 Feb 2025, ~1 month internal review, demos by end March/April, then 'if we decide to go with an RFP, it might be launched in 2025 calendar year'; deployment and IT strategy would decide which module is bought/implemented first; budget still needs to be consolidated.

Cooling signal: last buyer reply 23 Apr 2026 (Benjamin Zhu: 'Is Order Management part of any module in the proposal?'); rep asked him to define Order Management on 24 Apr, followed up 03 May with an AI Virtual Global Summit invite to Benjamin Zhu and Miranda Carington and an order-management follow-up, all with no logged buyer response. No recorded buyer call in over 12 months.

Enterprise scope and roster: $550k full-suite deal (iContract, iSource, ML iAnalyze, iSupplier, iSaaS, Merlin, iRisk, Merlin Intake) serving ~120 core sourcing/category managers plus ~6,000 TTL project managers across IBRD, IFC, MIGA and ICSID. Contact roles: Kenny K. Cheung (Chief of Procurement and Director - Budget, Performance Review & Strategic Planning), Miranda Carington (Project Manager), Timothy Gross (Global Category Team Head, Corporate Services & Real Estate, Corporate Procurement), Gallina Vincelette (Vice President).
north_star_verdict.headline: World Bank Group source-to-pay transformation stalled in Qualified: strong 2025 RFI/demo cycle with positive AI and Teams feedback, but the only engaged thread (Benjamin Zhu) has gone quiet since a 23 Apr 2026 order-management question, with the promised 2025 RFP never launched.

flags: Convert Benjamin Zhu from neutral evaluator to active champion: his AI/Teams feedback is positive but he has gone silent for ~70 days and holds no proven budget access.
Break single-threading by reaching Kenny K. Cheung (Chief of Procurement, budget owner) and Timothy Gross (category team head) before the RFP defines terms.
Surface the undisclosed competitive shortlist: this is an explicit multi-vendor RFI, so ask Benjamin Zhu who else advanced to build a real differentiation play.
best_practice_check.summary: This is a large, single-threaded RFI-stage deal that has gone quiet; the win levers are converting the one engaged evaluator into a champion, reaching real power before the RFP, and surfacing the competitive field.

customer_preference.evidence: Positive but non-decisive: 'we use Teams very much, so this is definitely an interesting solution' and 'the AI component was something interesting' (01 Jul 2025), stated as an off-the-record initial impression during a non-selection RFI; no recent (2026) re-confirmation, so this signal is decaying.

competitive_position.summary: No competitor is named in any source: Competitors__c is blank and no rival appears in the two transcripts, next steps, tasks or field history. This is NOT a sole-source deal, though: Benjamin Zhu ran a deliberate multi-vendor RFI ('a market exploration', 'who offers what', 'we cannot afford inviting everyone to the demo', 03 Feb 2025), so Zycus is competing against an undisclosed set. Zycus cleared into two detailed demos and drew positive feedback on Merlin AI and Microsoft Teams-native intake ('we use Teams very much... the AI component was something interesting', 01 Jul 2025), indicating strong RFI positioning. The single most important competitive action is to surface the shortlist by asking Benjamin Zhu who else advanced; until then the current threat is UNKNOWN and no rival should be asserted. Zycus wedge: Merlin Intake for Teams, the autonomous-negotiation agent, and full-suite breadth covering all six RFI functionalities versus likely point-solution rivals.

items.grounding_quote: Ben, please define Order Management in your requirements. Are you referring to Order to Cash, as in fulfilling incoming orders?

ai_positioning_strength.summary: AI/Merlin positioning is landing: the buyer independently flagged the AI component as interesting and the Teams-native intake as a strong fit, and the RFI itself lists AI-driven demand forecasting and ML classification. The next lever is to convert that interest into a concrete, quantified agentic story (Merlin Intake for Teams, autonomous negotiation) put in front of power.
